items.mistake: Confusing average rate of change with instantaneous derivative
items.fix: Use (f(b)-f(a))/(b-a) only for average slope; derivative is the limit as the interval shrinks.
items.why: Average slope over a wide interval can hide sharp local behavior.
items.example: f(x)=|x| has average slope 0 on [-1,1] but no derivative at 0.

items.mistake: Applying the product rule as (fg)′ = f′g′
items.why: The wrong rule gives incorrect gradients and broken backprop for multiplied terms.
items.example: For f(x)=x, g(x)=x, correct derivative is 2x; f′g′ would wrongly give 1.

items.mistake: Using too large a finite-difference step h
items.fix: Try h around 1e-5 to 1e-7 for float64; compare central vs forward differences.
items.why: Large h gives truncation error; tiny h gives rounding error.
items.example: h=1e-2 on sin(x) at x=1 may error by ~1e-4; h=1e-6 is usually closer to cos(1).
